question: What is the main purpose of mankind?
prayer: Dear God, thank You for making us to know You and enjoy You forever. Help us to live every day in a way that shows how great You are, and to find our greatest happiness in You. Amen.

question: What rule has God given to direct us?
prayer: Thank You, God, for giving us the Bible to show us how to know You and live for You. Help us to love Your Word and learn from it every day. Amen.

question: What do the Scriptures primarily teach?
prayer: Thank You, Lord, that the Bible teaches us who You are and what You want us to do. Help us to keep learning about You and to gladly do what You say. Amen.

question: What is God?
prayer: Dear God, You are so amazing — You have always been alive, You never change, and You are perfectly wise, powerful, holy, and good. We worship You because there is no one else like You. Amen.

question: Are there more gods than one?
prayer: Thank You, Lord, that there is only one true and living God — You! Help us never to be fooled into putting anything or anyone else in Your place. Amen.

question: How many persons are there in the Godhead?
prayer: Dear God, thank You for being Father, Son, and Holy Spirit — three persons, one God, equally great and glorious. We praise You for being so wonderful and beyond what we can fully understand. Amen.

question: What are the decrees of God?
prayer: Lord, thank You that nothing happens by accident — You have a perfect plan for everything, and You are working it all out for Your glory. Help us to trust Your plan even when we don't understand it. Amen.

question: How does God carry out his decrees?
prayer: Dear God, thank You that You carry out Your great plans through creating and caring for everything You have made. We praise You for being in complete control of all things. Amen.

question: What is the work of creation?
prayer: Thank You, God, for making everything out of nothing — the sky, the sea, the animals, and us! Help us always to remember that You are the Creator of all good things. Amen.

question: How did God create mankind?
prayer: Thank You, Lord, for making people in Your image — with the ability to know You, do what is right, and care for Your world. Help us to remember how special You made us to be. Amen.

question: What are God's works of providence?
prayer: Dear God, thank You that You don't just make things and walk away — You care for everything You have made, every single day, with wisdom, power, and love. Thank You for watching over us. Amen.

question: What special act of providence did God exercise toward mankind in creation?
prayer: Lord, thank You that from the very beginning You entered into a special agreement with people, promising life for perfect obedience. Help us to understand how much You love to bless those who follow You. Amen.

question: Did our first parents continue in the state in which they were created?
prayer: Dear God, we are sorry that Adam and Eve chose to disobey You, and that we also do wrong things every day. Please forgive us, and thank You that You did not leave us without hope. Amen.

question: What is sin?
prayer: Lord, help us to understand that sin is anything that breaks Your law or falls short of what You have commanded. We are sorry for all the ways we sin, and we ask You to forgive us for Jesus's sake. Amen.

question: What was the sin by which our first parents fell?
prayer: Dear God, we are sorry that our first parents chose to disobey You by eating the forbidden fruit. Help us to choose to obey You even when it is hard and when disobedience seems tempting. Amen.

question: Did all mankind fall in Adam's first transgression?
prayer: Lord, help us to understand that because Adam was our representative, his sin affected all of us. We confess that we, too, are sinners, and we need Your help and rescue. Amen.

question: What state did the fall bring for mankind?
prayer: Dear God, help us to see how serious sin is — that it brought the whole world into sin and misery. Please make us run to You for rescue rather than thinking we can fix things ourselves. Amen.

question: What does the sinfulness of mankind's fallen state consist of?
prayer: Lord, we confess that we are born with sin already inside us, and that we do wrong things every day because of it. Please clean us on the inside and make us more like You. Amen.

question: What is the misery of mankind's fallen state?
prayer: Dear God, help us to understand how terrible it would be to be separated from You forever. We don't want to live under Your anger — please bring us close to You through Jesus. Amen.

question: Did God leave all mankind to perish in the state of sin and misery?
prayer: Thank You, God, that You didn't leave all people to be lost forever! Before the world even began, You chose some people for everlasting life and planned to send a Redeemer to save them. Thank You for Your amazing grace. Amen.

question: Who is the Redeemer of God's elect?
prayer: Thank You, Lord Jesus, for being our Redeemer — fully God and fully human at the same time — so that You could rescue us! We praise You that You are still both God and man forever. Amen.

question: How did Christ, being the Son of God, become man?
prayer: Dear Lord Jesus, thank You for becoming a real person — for being born as a baby to the Virgin Mary, by the power of the Holy Spirit — so that You could live and die in our place. We are amazed at Your love. Amen.

question: What offices does Christ carry out as our Redeemer?
prayer: Thank You, Jesus, for being our prophet who teaches us, our priest who sacrificed Himself for us, and our king who rules and protects us. We praise You for being everything we could ever need. Amen.

question: How does Christ carry out the office of a prophet?
prayer: Dear Lord Jesus, thank You for being our teacher and prophet — revealing to us through Your Word and Spirit exactly what God wants for us and how we can be saved. Help us always to listen to You. Amen.

question: How does Christ carry out the office of a priest?
prayer: Thank You, Jesus, for offering Yourself as the perfect sacrifice for our sins, and for always praying for us before Your Father. We are so grateful that You have made peace between us and God. Amen.

question: How does Christ carry out the office of a king?
prayer: Dear Lord Jesus, thank You for being our King — for changing our hearts so that we want to follow You, for protecting us every day, and for one day winning the final victory over every enemy. Amen.

question: What did Christ's humiliation consist of?
prayer: Lord Jesus, thank You for humbling Yourself so deeply for us — being born in a lowly place, living under the law, suffering, dying on the cross, and being buried. We can hardly believe how much You gave up because You love us. Amen.

question: What does Christ's exaltation consist of?
prayer: Dear Lord Jesus, thank You that You rose from the dead, went up to heaven, and now sit at the right hand of the Father. We praise You that one day You will come back to judge the world, and everyone will see Your glory. Amen.

question: How are we made partakers of the redemption purchased by Christ?
prayer: Thank You, God, for sending Your Holy Spirit to give us personally everything that Jesus won for us on the cross. Help us to experience and treasure all the blessings of Christ's redemption. Amen.

question: How does the Spirit apply the redemption purchased by Christ to us?
prayer: Dear Holy Spirit, thank You for creating faith in our hearts and uniting us to Jesus. We praise You for calling us to come to Christ and bringing us into His family. Amen.

question: What is effectual calling?
prayer: Lord, thank You that by Your Spirit You show us our sin, open our eyes to who Jesus is, and give us new hearts that are willing and glad to come to Him. Please keep working in us so that we love You more and more every day. Amen.

question: What benefits do those who are effectually called partake of in this life?
prayer: Thank You, God, for the wonderful gifts You give to those You call — being made right with You, being welcomed into Your family, and being made more and more holy. We are so grateful for all these blessings. Amen.

question: What is justification?
prayer: Dear God, thank You for justification — for completely forgiving all our sins and treating us as righteous, not because of anything we have done, but entirely because of what Jesus has done for us. Help us never to take this amazing gift for granted. Amen.

question: What is adoption?
prayer: Thank You, Father, for adopting us into Your family and making us Your very own children. Help us to live as children who know they are loved, and who love You back with all their hearts. Amen.

question: What is sanctification?
prayer: Dear God, thank You for the gift of sanctification — for making us more like You from the inside out. Please keep changing us so that we love sin less and love righteousness more with every passing day. Amen.

question: What are the benefits that accompany justification, adoption, and sanctification in this life?
prayer: Thank You, Lord, for all the wonderful things that flow from being justified, adopted, and sanctified — knowing that You love us, having peace in our hearts, feeling joy in the Holy Spirit, and growing in grace every day. Help us to treasure these gifts. Amen.

question: What benefits do believers receive from Christ at death?
prayer: Dear God, thank You that when believers die, their souls go straight to be with You in perfect holiness, and that even their bodies remain united to Jesus until the resurrection. Death is not the end — what a wonderful comfort! Amen.

question: What benefits do believers receive from Christ at the resurrection?
prayer: Thank You, Lord, for the glorious hope of the resurrection — that believers will be raised up in glory, declared innocent on judgment day, and enjoy Your presence perfectly and forever. We can't wait! Amen.

question: What is the duty that God requires of mankind?
prayer: Dear God, help us to understand that You require us to obey everything You have told us in Your Word. Please give us hearts that truly want to do Your will, and the strength to actually do it. Amen.

question: What did God first reveal to mankind as the rule of their obedience?
prayer: Thank You, God, for giving us the moral law to clearly show us how You want us to live. Help us to take Your law seriously and to love what You love. Amen.

question: Where is the moral law summarized?
prayer: Thank You, Lord, for summarizing Your law so clearly in the Ten Commandments. Help us to learn them well so that we know what it truly means to live in a way that pleases You. Amen.

question: What is the summary of the Ten Commandments?
prayer: Dear God, help us to love You with everything we have — our whole heart, soul, mind, and strength — and to love the people around us as much as we love ourselves. We know we can't do this without Your help. Amen.

question: What is the preface to the Ten Commandments?
prayer: Thank You, God, for reminding us at the very start of the commandments who You are — our Lord and Redeemer who rescued Your people from slavery. Help us never to forget all You have done for us. Amen.

question: What does the preface to the Ten Commandments teach us?
prayer: Lord, because You are our God and You have redeemed us, help us to gladly keep all Your commandments — not to earn Your love, but because we already have it. Amen.

question: What is the first commandment?
prayer: Dear God, help us to have no other gods before You — not money, not popularity, not comfort, not anything. You alone are the true God, and You alone deserve our full worship. Amen.

question: What is required in the first commandment?
prayer: Lord, help us to know You as the one true God, to worship You from the heart, and to give You all the glory that only You deserve — in everything we do. Amen.

question: What is forbidden in the first commandment?
prayer: Dear God, please keep us from ever turning away from You or giving to anyone or anything the worship and honor that belongs to You alone. You are our God, and we are Yours. Amen.

question: What are we especially taught by the words "before me" in the first commandment?
prayer: Lord, remind us that You see everything — every thought, every choice, every hidden thing. Help us to fear displeasing You by setting up any idol in our hearts, because You are always watching and You care deeply. Amen.

question: What is the second commandment?
prayer: Dear God, help us never to make images or pictures to represent You or to worship You in ways You haven't commanded. Teach us to worship You the way You actually want to be worshiped. Amen.

question: What is required in the second commandment?
prayer: Lord, help us to receive, follow, and protect everything You have commanded about worship — keeping it pure and complete, not adding our own ideas and not taking anything away. Amen.

question: What is forbidden in the second commandment?
prayer: Dear God, please keep us from worshiping You through idols or in ways You haven't approved in Your Word. Help us to honor You the way You actually want to be honored. Amen.

question: What are the reasons added to the second commandment?
prayer: Thank You, Lord, that You care so much about how we worship You — because You are our sovereign Lord, our owner, and You are passionately jealous for Your own glory. Help us to share Your passion for pure and true worship. Amen.

question: What is the third commandment?
prayer: Dear God, help us never to use Your name carelessly, emptily, or dishonestly. Your name is holy, and we want to treat it with the deep respect it deserves. Amen.

question: What is required in the third commandment?
prayer: Lord, teach us to use Your name, Your titles, Your Word, and everything about You with reverence and care. May everything we say about You be true and truly honoring to You. Amen.

question: What is forbidden in the third commandment?
prayer: Dear God, help us never to dishonor or misuse Your name, Your Word, or anything by which You reveal Yourself. Guard our lips and our hearts so that we always speak of You rightly. Amen.

question: What is the reason added to the third commandment?
prayer: Lord, even when no one else sees or cares about our sin, You see everything and You are perfectly just. Help us to fear dishonoring Your name, knowing that You will not let it go unpunished forever. Amen.

question: Which is the fourth commandment?
prayer: Thank You, God, for giving us the gift of the Sabbath — a whole day set apart to rest and to focus on You. Help us to treasure this gift and to look forward to it, rather than treating it as a burden. Amen.

question: What is required in the fourth commandment?
prayer: Dear Lord, help us to keep one day in seven as truly holy to You — resting from regular work and really worshiping, not just going through the motions. Help us to do this gladly. Amen.

question: Which day of the seven has God appointed to be the weekly Sabbath?
prayer: Thank You, Lord, for giving us Sunday — the Lord's Day — as our Sabbath, to celebrate the resurrection of Jesus on the first day of the week. Help us to honor this day joyfully. Amen.

question: How is the Sabbath to be sanctified?
prayer: Dear God, on Your special day please help us to set aside our usual work and play, and to spend the time in worship, prayer, hearing Your Word, and resting in You. May we use this day the way You intended. Amen.

question: What is forbidden in the fourth commandment?
prayer: Lord, on the Lord's Day help us not to be lazy or careless, not to fill the day with sin, and not to let our minds keep drifting back to work and play when they should be focused on You. Amen.

question: What are the reasons added to the fourth commandment?
prayer: Thank You, God, for giving us six whole days for our ordinary activities, and for claiming just one special day as Yours. Thank You for setting the example Yourself in creation and for blessing the Sabbath day. Amen.

question: What is the fifth commandment?
prayer: Thank You, God, for giving us parents and other people in authority to care for and guide us. Help us to be grateful for them and to treat them well. Amen.

question: What is required in the fifth commandment?
prayer: Dear God, help us to honor and respect our parents, teachers, and others in authority over us, and to treat everyone — whether above us, below us, or equal to us — the way they deserve to be treated. Amen.

question: What is forbidden in the fifth commandment?
prayer: Lord, please keep us from being disrespectful or disobedient to those You have placed over us, and from failing in our duties toward anyone You have put in our lives. Help us to honor them as You command. Amen.

question: What is the reason added to the fifth commandment?
prayer: Thank You, God, for the wonderful promise attached to the fifth commandment — that those who honor their parents will be blessed with long and flourishing lives, as You see fit for Your glory and their good. Amen.

question: What is the sixth commandment?
prayer: Dear God, You have commanded us not to murder. Help us to care deeply for our own life and the lives of others, knowing that every person is made in Your image and is precious to You. Amen.

question: What is required in the sixth commandment?
prayer: Lord, help us to do everything we can to keep ourselves and others safe and healthy. Teach us to treat every human life as the precious gift from You that it truly is. Amen.

question: What is forbidden in the sixth commandment?
prayer: Dear God, help us never to do anything that would harm or destroy the life of another person — or even our own life. May we always work to protect and care for those around us. Amen.

question: What is the seventh commandment?
prayer: Lord, You have commanded us to be faithful and pure. Help us to take this seriously and to honor this commandment in our hearts, our words, and our behavior every day. Amen.

question: What is required in the seventh commandment?
prayer: Dear God, help us to be pure in our hearts, our words, and our actions, and to genuinely care about the purity of those around us too. Give us the strength and desire to say no to anything that leads us away from this. Amen.

question: What is forbidden in the seventh commandment?
prayer: Lord, please guard our minds and hearts against impure thoughts. Help us to turn away from anything — images, words, or actions — that dishonors this commandment and grieves You. Amen.

question: What is the eighth commandment?
prayer: Dear God, help us never to take what doesn't belong to us. Teach us to be honest and to deeply respect what You have given to other people. Amen.

question: What is required in the eighth commandment?
prayer: Lord, help us to work honestly and to use the good things You have given us wisely. Teach us also to care about the wellbeing of others and not to think only of ourselves. Amen.

question: What is forbidden in the eighth commandment?
prayer: Dear God, please help us never to cheat, steal, or do anything dishonest that hurts ourselves or others. Give us hearts that are truly and cheerfully honest. Amen.

question: What is the ninth commandment?
prayer: Lord, You have told us not to give false testimony about others. Help us to be people who always speak the truth and who treat everyone fairly, especially when it costs us something. Amen.

question: What is required in the ninth commandment?
prayer: Dear God, help us always to be truthful, to stand up for what is right, and to protect the good name of others — especially when it would be easy to tear them down or stay silent. Amen.

question: What is forbidden in the ninth commandment?
prayer: Lord, please keep us from lying, exaggerating, or saying anything that harms our own or someone else's reputation. Help us to love truth the way You do. Amen.

question: What is the tenth commandment?
prayer: Dear God, You have commanded us not to want what belongs to others. Help us to be truly satisfied with what You have given us and not to wish we had what belongs to someone else. Amen.

question: What is required in the tenth commandment?
prayer: Lord, please give us truly content hearts — hearts that are happy with what You have provided and that genuinely wish good things for the people around us. Amen.

question: What is forbidden in the tenth commandment?
prayer: Dear God, help us not to be grumpy or jealous when others are blessed with good things. Please take away any envy or greed in our hearts, and fill us with gratitude and love instead. Amen.

question: Is anyone able to keep the commandments of God perfectly?
prayer: Lord, we confess that we fail to keep Your commandments every single day — in what we think, say, and do. We cannot do it on our own. Please forgive us for Jesus's sake, and help us to keep turning back to You. Amen.

question: Are all transgressions of the law equally evil?
prayer: Dear God, help us to understand that some sins are worse than others, and that all sin is truly serious. Give us wisdom to hate what You hate, and to be especially careful about things that grieve You most deeply. Amen.

question: What does every sin deserve?
prayer: Lord, we know that every sin deserves Your anger and punishment, in this life and the next. Thank You that Jesus took that punishment in our place. Help us never to treat sin as a small or unimportant thing. Amen.

question: What does God require of us to escape his wrath and curse?
prayer: Dear God, please give us true faith in Jesus, a genuine sorrow for our sins that leads to real change, and help us to faithfully use all the means You have given us to grow — Your Word, prayer, and the sacraments. Amen.

question: What is faith in Jesus Christ?
prayer: Thank You, God, for the gift of saving faith — a faith that rests completely on Jesus and trusts Him alone for salvation. Please give this faith to us and to all who have not yet believed, and help us to hold onto it firmly. Amen.

question: What is repentance that leads to life?
prayer: Dear God, please give us true repentance — a real sadness for our sins because they are against You, and a genuine turning away from them back to You. Help us to keep turning to You every single day. Amen.

question: What are the outward and ordinary means by which Christ imparts to us the benefits of redemption?
prayer: Thank You, Lord, for giving us regular, everyday ways to receive Your blessings — Your Word, baptism, the Lord's Supper, and prayer. Help us to use these gifts faithfully and never to take them for granted. Amen.

question: How is the Word made effectual to salvation?
prayer: Thank You, God, that when Your Word is read, and especially when it is preached, Your Spirit uses it to bring people to faith and help them grow in holiness. Help us to love hearing Your Word and to really listen when we do. Amen.

question: How is the Word to be read and heard that it may become effectual to salvation?
prayer: Dear God, when we read or hear Your Word, help us to pay close attention, to prepare our hearts beforehand, to pray for understanding, and then to actually do what it says. Don't let Your Word bounce off us without changing us. Amen.

question: How do the sacraments become effectual means of salvation?
prayer: Thank You, Lord, that the sacraments work not because of anything magical in them or because of anything we have done, but only because of Jesus's blessing and the Holy Spirit's work in those who receive them with faith. Amen.

question: What is a sacrament?
prayer: Dear God, thank You for the sacraments — holy signs that Christ Himself gave to His church to show and seal all the blessings of the new covenant to those who believe. Help us to treasure them rightly. Amen.

question: Which are the sacraments of the New Testament?
prayer: Thank You, Lord, for baptism and the Lord's Supper — the two sacraments You gave to Your church. Help us to understand what they mean and to receive them with faith and gratitude. Amen.

question: What is baptism?
prayer: Dear God, thank You for baptism — a wonderful sign that pictures being washed clean, being joined to Jesus, and being promised all the blessings of Your covenant. Help us to remember and treasure what baptism means. Amen.

question: To whom is baptism to be administered?
prayer: Thank You, Lord, for the gift of being raised in a covenant family where children receive the sign of baptism and grow up hearing about Jesus. Please help the children of believers truly come to know and love You for themselves. Amen.

question: What is the Lord's Supper?
prayer: Dear Lord, thank You for the Lord's Supper — where through bread and wine we remember Jesus's death, proclaim it to others, and truly feed on Him by faith for our souls' nourishment. Help us to come to Your table with grateful and believing hearts. Amen.

question: What is required for receiving the Lord's Supper in a worthy manner?
prayer: Lord, before we come to Your table, help us to examine ourselves honestly — to check that we truly understand what we are doing, that we trust Jesus, and that we are genuinely sorry for sin and wanting to live differently. Don't let us ever come carelessly. Amen.

question: What is prayer?
prayer: Dear God, thank You that You invite us to bring our desires and needs to You — in Jesus's name, confessing our sins, and thanking You for Your kindness. Help us to pray often and to mean what we say. Amen.

question: What rule has God given for our direction in prayer?
prayer: Thank You, Lord, for giving us the Lord's Prayer as a special guide for how to pray. Help us to use it to shape our hearts and prayers — not just as words to repeat, but as a wonderful pattern to follow. Amen.

question: What does the preface of the Lord's Prayer teach us?
prayer: Dear Father in heaven, when we pray, help us to come to You with both deep reverence and confident trust — knowing that You are holy and great, but also that You are our loving Father who is ready and able to help us. And help us to pray for others, not only for ourselves. Amen.

question: What do we pray for in the first petition?
prayer: Dear God, may Your name be treated as holy — by us, and by all people everywhere. Help us and others to glorify You in everything we do, so that all of life points back to Your glory. Amen.

question: What do we pray for in the second petition?
prayer: Lord, please destroy the kingdom of darkness and advance Your kingdom of grace — bring more and more people to know and love You. And please come quickly, King Jesus, to bring Your kingdom of glory in all its fullness. Amen.

question: What do we pray for in the third petition?
prayer: Dear Father, please help us to know and want and do Your will in all things — just as the angels in heaven do it perfectly. Help us to fully trust You and to submit gladly to everything You have planned. Amen.

question: What do we pray for in the fourth petition?
prayer: Lord, thank You for providing food, clothing, and everything we need for life. Please give us what we need today, and help us to receive every good thing from Your hand with gratitude and not to worry. Amen.

question: What do we pray for in the fifth petition?
prayer: Dear Father, please forgive all our sins for Jesus's sake. And help us to truly forgive everyone who has hurt or wronged us, so that our hearts stay soft and free from bitterness. Amen.

question: What do we pray for in the sixth petition?
prayer: Lord, please keep us away from temptation and protect us from falling into sin. And when temptation does come, please make us strong and rescue us from the evil one. Amen.

question: What does the conclusion of the Lord's Prayer teach us?
prayer: Dear Father, we praise You — because the kingdom, the power, and the glory are all Yours, forever! We trust that You hear our prayers, and we say Amen — meaning "Yes, Lord, let it be so!" with all our hearts. Amen.
